meaning: adj.压制性的,压迫的,沉重的,难以忍受的
examples: An excess of house plants in a small apartment can be oppressive.
Perhaps in the face of impersonal and intimidating globalization, a young person's family feels more like a friendly haven than an oppressive trap.
The oppressive afternoon heat had tired him out.
Although roofless, it feels oppressive: a tight rectangle of low walls and odd sculptures.
The reformer Ida Tarbell went to Highland Park planning to expose the oppressive Ford system.
